title: Understanding Average Acceleration
text: To find the average acceleration, you need to know the change in velocity and the time over which this change occurs.

title: Formula for Average Acceleration
text: The formula for average acceleration is given by: $$a=\frac{v_f-v_i}{t}$$, where $$v_f$$ is the final velocity, $$v_i$$ is the initial velocity, and $$t$$ is the time taken.

title: Initial and Final Velocities
text: Since the motorcycle starts from rest, the initial velocity $$v_i$$ is $$0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$. The final velocity $$v_f$$ is given as $$26.8$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.

title: Calculate the Change in Velocity
text: Calculate the change in velocity using the formula: $$v_f-v_i$$.

title: Calculate the Average Acceleration
text: Now, use the formula $$a=\frac{v_f-v_i}{t}$$ to find the average acceleration. Substitute $$v_f=26.8$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, $$v_i=0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, and $$t=3.90$$ s.
